name: The Stars Route - Fleurance Loop
name_original: Fleurance et Lavardens – boucle dans le Pays d'Auch
description: Difficult 47.5-mile touring cycling loop in Pays d'Auch, featuring historic villages and vineyard views, with 2588 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Fleurance traverse the rolling countryside of the Gers department in southwest France, characterized by undulating hills and scenic beauty. The region, often referred to as Gascony, offers varied terrain suitable for cycling. Fleurance is situated amidst a verdant environment, featuring serene lakes and picturesque landscapes. Best touring cycling routes around Fleurance The most popular touring cycling route is The Stars Route - Fleurance Loop, a 47.5 miles (76.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 45 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through the region's undulating terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The Fleurance Market Hall loop from Fleurance, a difficult 27.1 miles (43.6 km) path. This route explores the local countryside and passes through historical areas around Fleurance. Local touring cyclists also love the Lectoure loop from Fleurance, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail leading through rolling hills and rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours. Touring cycling around Fleurance is defined by rolling countryside, undulating hills, and serene lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Fleurance are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 1900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Fleurance's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Fleurance?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes available around Fleurance, catering to various skill levels. The region offers a diverse network of paths through its rolling countryside.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Fleurance?
A: The routes around Fleurance offer a good mix of difficulties. You'll find 3 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 13 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in Fleurance?
A: Yes, Fleurance offers several challenging touring cycling routes, characterized by significant elevation gain through the region's undulating terrain. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021109833/the-stars-route-fleurance-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Stars Route - Fleurance Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 76 kilometers with considerable climbing.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Fleurance?
A: While many routes feature rolling hills, there are easier options available. These routes typically have less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Look for routes marked as 'easy' or 'moderate' on Komoot.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Fleurance traverse the serene and verdant Gascony countryside, characterized by undulating hills and picturesque landscapes. You'll encounter tranquil lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3201029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys&lt;/a&gt;, and enjoy exceptionally pure and clear skies, especially at night.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Fleurance, as a bastide town, features a historic central square with a 19th-century market hall. You can also visit the Gothic-style Church of Saint Laurent. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10609485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Fleurance Market Hall loop from Fleurance&lt;/a&gt; explore these historical areas. Additionally, the region is home to several castles and charming villages.

Q: Can I visit any castles while cycling near Fleurance?
A: Yes, the area around Fleurance is rich in historical sites, including several castles. You might pass by landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5681633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Terraube&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's past. For more, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3830246/castles-around-fleurance" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles around Fleurance guide&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Fleurance?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Fleurance are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021109833/the-stars-route-fleurance-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Stars Route - Fleurance Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35630427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure loop from Fleurance&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Fleurance?
A: The area is highly rated by the Komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful rolling countryside, and the opportunity to explore charming historical areas and serene lakes.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Fleurance?
A: Yes, Fleurance, being a welcoming town for visitors, offers various parking options. You can typically find parking in the town center, especially near the Central Square, which provides convenient access to many of the cycling routes that start or pass through the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Fleurance?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Fleurance, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, but the clear skies are ideal for enjoying the natural beauty, especially around the lakes.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes in Fleurance suitable for families?
A: While many routes involve undulating terrain, there are easier and shorter options that can be suitable for families, especially those with older children or those using e-bikes. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances for a more family-friendly experience.
tips: Former military capital of the Counts of Armagnac, classified among the "most beautiful villages in France". Its castle, perched on a rocky outcrop dominates green valleys.

The bastide of Fleurance was founded in the 13th century. The central arcaded square hosts a beautiful 19th century hall. At the end of July, the Cuivro foliz, a festival of street brass bands, takes place there.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Fleurance and Lavardens loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 76.4 km long and generally takes around 4 hours and 45 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your cycling speed, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery and points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of paved roads, with approximately 65.4 km of asphalt and 11.1 km of tarmac. You can expect a landscape of rolling hills characteristic of Gascony, meaning there will be a number of climbs. While not overly mountainous, a good touring bicycle with at least 20 gears or an electric bike is recommended for easier ascents.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this route as 'difficult' due to its distance and significant elevation gain of nearly 789 meters. While the terrain is mostly paved, the rolling hills require good fitness. Beginners might find it challenging, and an electric bike could make the ascents more manageable.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich tapestry of historical and natural sights. Key highlights include the charming Lavardens Village with its imposing Château de Lavardens, and The Fleurance Market Hall in the central square of Fleurance. You'll also cycle through picturesque villages, medieval bastides, and enjoy beautiful views over Armagnac vineyards, characteristic farms, windmills, and the verdant Réjaumont forest.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Pays d'Auch region is known for its sunflowers, suggesting late spring to early autumn (May to September) would be ideal. During these months, you can enjoy pleasant weather and the vibrant agricultural landscapes. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, Fleurance serves as a primary starting point for this loop. You should be able to find parking within Fleurance, particularly near the town center or market hall, to begin your cycling adventure.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a touring bicycle route predominantly on paved roads, it can be dog-friendly if your dog is accustomed to cycling alongside you or can comfortably ride in a bicycle trailer. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort, especially on longer distances and in varying temperatures.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public touring bicycle route in the Pays d'Auch region. You are free to enjoy the scenic roads and villages at your leisure.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with or runs alongside sections of several other trails. These include parts of the Cœur de Gascogne, Circuit VTT n°11, Circuit VTT n°12, Circuit VTT n°19, Circuit VTT n°16, Circuit VTT n°18, and Circuit VTT n°17.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and elevation, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, and appropriate cycling gear. A touring bicycle with at least 20 gears is recommended, or an e-bike for easier climbs. Don't forget sun protection, a basic repair kit, and a fully charged phone for navigation and emergencies.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the Fleurance and Lavardens loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several picturesque villages and bastides, including Fleurance and Lavardens, you will find opportunities for refreshments. These towns typically offer cafes, bakeries, and restaurants where you can stop for a break and enjoy local Gascon cuisine.
